column9: Parent: [[fss-bok-unrestricted-mou-template-and-bot-act-1942-bahtnet]]; CRITICAL LEGAL FINDING: DPA Act Section 39 confirmed text: BOT or other supervisory authorities and DPA "may provide or exchange examination reports or OTHER INFORMATION REGARDING FINANCIAL INSTITUTIONS upon request in order to monitor financial institutions standings and performance"; "other information" is broader than examination reports; BAHTNET outflow data of CLICX = "information regarding [CLICX as] financial institution" = strong interpretive argument that Section 39 already covers BAHTNET data; Section 40 finding: DPA can DIRECTLY require financial institutions (including CLICX) to submit periodic or occasional confidential reports — ALTERNATIVE PATH bypassing BOT/BAHTNET entirely; Royal Decree route RULED OUT: Royal Decree authority in DPA Act is ENUMERATED specifically for (1) premium rates and (2) adding specialized FIs to coverage; NO general Royal Decree authority for information sharing exists in DPA Act; Payment Systems Act B.E. 2560 (2017): effective April 2018, gives BOT authority to collect data from payment operators "in any form"; feeds into Section 39 "other information" interpretation; payment system operator data ← BOT under PSA 2017 is "other information regarding FIs" arguably; Timeline comparison: MOU (administrative, weeks) > Royal Decree (Cabinet, 3-6 months IF authority exists — it does not) > DPA Act amendment (Parliament, 12-24 months); PDPA exemption: aggregate BAHTNET data (non-personal) bypasses PDPA entirely; contested: whether "other information" is contextually limited by ejusdem generis (same type as examination reports) vs. broadly literal; contested: Section 40 timeliness — DPA can require periodic reports but not real-time; 4 new queue entries (Section 39 scope score 11, Section 40 direct CLICX path score 11, MOU operationalization score 10, Royal Decree enumeration constraint score 10)
